pi_items.source: Matches the body requirement dynamically created in lunar_game_project.json via setup_task.sh and the task prompt.
Matches the selenographic_latitude_deg requirement generated in lunar_game_project.json via setup_task.sh.
Matches the selenographic_longitude_deg requirement generated in lunar_game_project.json via setup_task.sh.
Calculated by converting 0.6741 degrees to radians: 0.6741 * pi / 180 = 0.011765.
Calculated by converting 23.4730 degrees to radians: 23.4730 * pi / 180 = 0.409680.
Matches the game_datetime_utc requirement generated in lunar_game_project.json via setup_task.sh.
Matches the time portion of the game_datetime_utc requirement generated in lunar_game_project.json via setup_task.sh.
Matches the display requirements explicitly defined in lunar_game_project.json and checked by export_result.sh via config.ini parsing.
Matches the exact output file path explicitly defined in the task description and verified in export_result.sh.
Matches the screenshot directory path checked by export_result.sh and the default Stellarium capture location on Linux.
Matches the exact number of screenshot capture steps instructed in the task description (Earth, Saturn, wide-field).

privileged_info_summary: Completed PI audit for stellarium_env lunar_game_sky_reference task by confirming that the prerequisite project data lunar_game_project.json is synthetically generated and dynamically injected via setup_task.sh. Verified that all metadata claims regarding targets (Moon), location coordinates (0.6741 lat, 23.4730 lon in deg and their radian equivalents), exact timestamp (2030-12-25 12:00:00 UTC), and expected config parameters explicitly match the task description instructions and setup scripts. Discovered that the task is defective because verifier.py is entirely missing from the environment, making the task completely unscorable programmatically, even though export_result.sh captures the state correctly.
